name: 114 Scott Ave
tagline: Historic warehouse party corridor. Dead-end near Morgan Ave L.
vibe: Graffiti warehouses, shipping containers, open paved lot. The Brooklyn warehouse party corridor (2005–2018).
setup: Against warehouse wall, facing the dead end. Sound directed away from Varick Ave.
exit: Multiple routes — crowd scatters toward Morgan Ave L in under 10 min. Metropolitan Ave, Flushing Ave also accessible.
why: Best transit, best zoning, most event-friendly corridor. 83rd Precinct deprioritizes industrial noise. Neighbors are acclimated to parties.
risks: Morgan Ave foot traffic = some visibility. 90th/83rd precinct boundary. Gentrification closing the window on these lots.

name: Red Hook Waterfront
tagline: Dead-end streets at the waterfront. Manhattan skyline + Statue of Liberty.
vibe: Fenced industrial lots, waterfront dead-ends. Pioneer Works corridor energy.
setup: Sullivan St preferred over Wolcott — better dispersal toward Van Brunt in multiple directions.
exit: Sullivan has multiple routes toward Van Brunt. Wolcott dead-ends at water = single exit, avoid.
why: Best views on the list. 76th Precinct covers huge area, slow response in Red Hook. No subway = natural crowd cap.
risks: No subway access. Dead-end dispersal on some streets. Coast Guard waterfront patrols. Cruise terminal security nearby.

name: Grand St / Newtown Creek
tagline: Industrial waterfront along Newtown Creek. Loading docks provide natural acoustic reinforcement.
vibe: Warehouses, light manufacturing. Three-sided sheds and loading docks for natural shade + sound.
setup: Use loading dock structures for shade and acoustic reinforcement. Set back from Grand St for less visibility.
exit: Grand St is a major thoroughfare — excellent dispersal in all directions.
why: Excellent transit, good dispersal, moderate environmental risk. 83rd Precinct, same low-priority approach as Scott Ave.
risks: More through-traffic on Grand St = more visible. Newtown Creek Superfund adjacency. 83rd/104th precinct boundary.

name: 151 Apollo St
tagline: Dead-end at Newtown Creek waterfront. Manhattan skyline views.
vibe: Industrial warehouses at a dead-end. Manhattan skyline views from the waterfront edge.
setup: Waterfront lots at end of Apollo St. Do NOT use generator — fuel on contaminated soil = federal liability.
exit: Single exit route back up Apollo to Meeker Ave. Weakest dispersal on the list.
why: Great views, heavy manufacturing zoning. Viable if other spots are blocked.
risks: Epicenter of Greenpoint oil spill (17-30M gallons). Explosive methane underground. 94th Precinct more responsive. EPA/Newtown Creek Alliance monitors active.
